story.iWant: the tool to automatically detect file formats from extensions and content
story.soThat: I don't have to manually specify the source format with --from flag every time
story.tasks: - Task 1: Create Format Detection Module (AC-1, AC-4) - Task 2: Implement Content-Based Detection (AC-2, AC-5) - Task 3: Implement Format Validation (AC-3, AC-6) - Task 4: Add Comprehensive Unit Tests (All ACs) - Task 5: Add Integration Tests (AC-5) - Task 6: Add Performance Benchmarks (AC-7) - Task 7: Update Documentation

acceptanceCriteria.ac: - detectFormat(filePath string) function accepts file path - Detects format from extension: .np3 → "np3", .xmp → "xmp", .lrtemplate → "lrtemplate" - Case-insensitive matching (.XMP = .xmp = .Xmp) - Returns error if extension is unrecognized - Returns error if file has no extension

- detectFormatFromBytes(data []byte) function accepts file content - Detects NP3: Magic bytes "NCP" (ASCII) at start + minimum 300 bytes - Detects XMP: XML structure (<?xml) + Camera Raw namespace (crs: or x:xmpmeta) - Detects lrtemplate: Lua table syntax (s = { at start after trimming whitespace) - Returns error if content doesn't match any known format - Used as fallback when extension detection fails or is ambiguous

- validateFormat(format string) function validates format identifier - Accepts: "np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate" - Rejects any other string with descriptive error - Used by convert command to validate user input

- Define format constants for type safety: const FormatNP3 = "np3", FormatXMP = "xmp", FormatLRTemplate = "lrtemplate" - Used throughout CLI codebase instead of string literals - Prevents typos and magic strings - Exported for use in other CLI modules

- Reuses converter.DetectFormat() for content-based detection - detectFormatFromBytes() wraps converter.DetectFormat() to maintain thin CLI layer - No format detection logic duplicated from internal/converter - CLI layer adds file path handling and extension detection

- Extension detection errors: "Unknown file format: {ext} (expected .np3, .xmp, or .lrtemplate)" - Content detection errors: "Unable to detect format from file content (size: {size} bytes)" - Validation errors: "Unsupported format: {format} (must be 'np3', 'xmp', or 'lrtemplate')" - Errors wrapped with helpful context for user

- Extension-based detection: <1ms (instant) - Content-based detection: <5ms for typical file (<50KB) - No performance regression from converter package - Minimal memory allocations (no unnecessary copying)

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 3 - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: cmd/cli/format.go - Format detection utilities. Provides detectFormat(filePath string) for extension-based detection and detectFormatFromBytes(data []byte) for content-based fallback. Returns format string ("np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate"). Used by convert command (Story 3-2) and batch command (Story 3-3). Performance: Extension <1ms, content <5ms.

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 3 - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Format detection APIs: detectFormat(filePath string) - extension-based, detectFormatFromBytes(data []byte) - content inspection using converter.DetectFormat(). validateFormat(format string) - validation helper. All maintain thin CLI layer by wrapping Epic 1 logic.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: CLI layer adds file path handling and extension detection, but defers to converter package for content-based detection. No business logic duplication. All format detection logic lives in internal/converter/converter.go DetectFormat() function.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: CLI detects input format automatically. User doesn't specify input format. CLI determines format from extension/content. Clear error if format ambiguous. Reduces cognitive load on user.

artifact.reason: Epic 1's proven format detection implementation. Content-based detection using magic bytes (NP3: "NCP"), XML namespace (XMP: "crs:" or "x:xmpmeta"), and Lua syntax (lrtemplate: "s = {"). This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for format detection - CLI wraps this function to maintain thin layer architecture.
Format constants defined in converter package. CLI should export matching constants for type safety and consistency. Values: "np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate".
Format validation helper in converter package. CLI can reuse this pattern for its own validation function. Checks if format string is one of supported values.
Example of wrapping converter.DetectFormat() for different interface (WASM). Shows pattern: validate args, call converter.DetectFormat(), wrap error. CLI should follow similar pattern.
Web interface format detection example. Calls WASM detectFormat() which wraps converter.DetectFormat(). Shows error handling pattern and user messaging.

stdlib.package: Ext() function for extension extraction, clean path handling
ReadFile() in tests to load real sample files
Standard Go test framework for unit and integration tests

internal.package: DetectFormat() function - CRITICAL dependency for content-based detection. Format constants (FormatNP3, FormatXMP, FormatLRTemplate).

architectural.constraint: Thin CLI Layer: Format detection must wrap converter.DetectFormat() for content-based detection. NO duplication of format detection logic from internal/converter.
Single Source of Truth: internal/converter/converter.go DetectFormat() is the ONLY place where magic bytes, XML parsing, and Lua syntax checking happens.
Type Safety: Export format constants (FormatNP3, FormatXMP, FormatLRTemplate) matching converter package values to prevent magic strings.
Performance: Extension-based detection must be <1ms (instant), content-based detection <5ms. Extension detection is preferred fast path.

implementation.constraint: Extension Priority: Always check file extension first before falling back to content inspection. Most users have correctly named files.
Case Insensitive: File extension matching must be case-insensitive (.XMP = .xmp = .Xmp) for user convenience.
Error Messages: All errors must be user-friendly with actionable suggestions. No technical jargon like "magic bytes" or "EOF".
No File I/O: Format detection functions should accept file path (string) or data ([]byte), never do file reading internally. Caller responsibility.

testing.constraint: Real Files: All tests must use real sample files from testdata/ directory. No synthetic test data.
Integration: CLI format detection must match converter package detection for same input. Validate with comparison tests.
Coverage: Achieve >95% code coverage. Test all three formats, case variations, unknown extensions, empty files, corrupted content.
Benchmarks: Performance targets validated via Go benchmarks. Extension <1μs, content <5ms.

interface.name: detectFormat
interface.description: Extension-based format detection (fast path). Extracts file extension using filepath.Ext(), converts to lowercase, maps to format constant. Returns error for unknown or missing extension.

interface.name: detectFormatFromBytes
interface.description: Content-based format detection (fallback). Wraps converter.DetectFormat() to maintain thin CLI layer. Used when extension is ambiguous or missing. Returns format string or error.

interface.name: validateFormat
interface.description: Format string validation. Accepts "np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate". Returns descriptive error for invalid formats with suggestions.

interface.name: converter.DetectFormat
interface.description: Core format detection logic from Epic 1. Examines magic bytes (NP3: "NCP"), XML structure (XMP: <?xml + crs:), Lua syntax (lrtemplate: s = {). This is the single source of truth - CLI wraps this for content detection.

interface.name: Format Constants
interface.signature: const FormatNP3 = "np3"; const FormatXMP = "xmp"; const FormatLRTemplate = "lrtemplate"
interface.description: Exported format identifiers for type safety. Match converter package constants exactly. Used by convert command and batch processing.

tests.standards: Go standard testing framework with table-driven tests. All format detection tests use real sample files from testdata/ directory (22 NP3, 913 XMP, 544 lrtemplate). Integration tests verify CLI detection matches converter package detection. Performance validated via Go benchmarks (extension <1μs, content <5ms). Coverage goal: >95% for format.go module.

locations.location: cmd/cli/format_test.go - Unit tests for extension detection, content detection, validation
testdata/np3/*.np3 - 22 real NP3 files for integration testing
testdata/xmp/*.xmp - 913 real XMP files for integration testing
testdata/lrtemplate/*.lrtemplate - 544 real lrtemplate files for integration testing

ideas.test: Extension-based detection: Test all three formats (.np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate), case variations (.XMP, .Xmp), unknown extensions (.txt, .jpg), missing extension (file without dot). Expected: Correct format returned for valid, error for invalid.

Content-based detection: Test NP3 magic bytes + size check, XMP XML structure + namespace, lrtemplate Lua syntax, unknown content, empty files. Expected: Correct format detected, error for unrecognized content.
Format validation: Test valid formats ("np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate"), invalid formats ("invalid", "", "NP3" uppercase). Expected: Valid formats pass, invalid rejected with clear error.
Format constants: Verify constants exist, have correct values, exported for use by other CLI modules. Expected: FormatNP3="np3", FormatXMP="xmp", FormatLRTemplate="lrtemplate".

Integration with converter: Verify detectFormatFromBytes() wraps converter.DetectFormat(), CLI detection matches converter results for same input. Use real files from testdata/. Expected: No code duplication, identical results.

Error messages: Test extension errors, content errors, validation errors. Verify messages are user-friendly, include suggestions. Expected: "Unknown file format: .txt (expected .np3, .xmp, or .lrtemplate)", "Unable to detect format from file content (size: X bytes)", "Unsupported format: X (must be 'np3', 'xmp', or 'lrtemplate')".
Performance: Benchmark extension detection (should be <1μs), content detection (should be <5ms). Run: go test -bench=. ./cmd/cli/. Expected: No performance regression vs converter package.
